title: 11 Herbal Essential Oils For Hangover
intro_desc: Herbal essential oils can be a great help when it comes to hangovers. You see, these oils are extracted from plants that have natural properties that can soothe and calm the body and mind. For instance, Zingiber officinale, also known as ginger, has anti-inflammatory properties that can help ease nausea and stomach discomfort. When you have a hangover, your stomach is usually upset and ginger oil can help calm it down. Another herb that's helpful is Lavandula angustifolia, or lavender. Its calming properties can help reduce anxiety and stress, which are common symptoms of a hangover. Valeriana officinalis, or valerian root, is another herb that's known for its calming effects. It can help you relax and reduce headaches, which are often caused by dehydration and stress. When you use these herbal essential oils, you can breathe in their soothing properties through your nose or apply them to your skin. This can help your body and mind recover from a hangover, and get you back to normal faster. By using these natural remedies, you can avoid relying on painkillers and other medications that can have negative side effects.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale essential oils contains gingerol and shogaol, which are active constituents that help alleviate hangover symptoms. Gingerol and shogaol have anti-inflammatory properties that reduce nausea and stomach discomfort. These compounds also have digestive properties that ease digestion and prevent stomach cramps. Additionally, gingerol and shogaol have antioxidant properties that help reduce oxidative stress and promote liver health. By using Zingiber officinale essential oils, you can potentially alleviate hangover symptoms and recover faster.
remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal essential oils for hangover
remedy_procedure: Gather 2 cups of fresh Zingiber officinale roots, 1 cup of carrier oil (coconut or olive oil), and a glass jar.
Clean and slice the Zingiber officinale roots into small pieces.
Fill the glass jar with the sliced roots, leaving 2 inches of space at the top.
Add 1 cup of carrier oil to the jar, making sure the roots are fully covered.
Steep the mixture in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day, then strain and store the oil in a dark container.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia essential oils contains active constituents like linalool and linalyl acetate. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help reduce the swelling and pain in the head and body associated with a hangover. The calming and soothing effects of linalool can also help alleviate stress and anxiety, common symptoms of a hangover. Additionally, linalyl acetate has been shown to have a sedative effect, which can help improve sleep quality and reduce fatigue. By reducing inflammation, alleviating stress, and promoting better sleep, Lavandula angustifolia essential oils may help alleviate hangover symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Lavandula angustifolia herbal essential oils for hangover
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Lavandula angustifolia flowers and 2 cups of water in a saucepan.
Heat the water over low heat and add the Lavandula angustifolia flowers. Let it simmer for 1 hour.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass bowl. Discard the solids.
Add 1 tablespoon of carrier oil (like jojoba oil) to the liquid and mix well.
Transfer the mixture to a dark glass bottle and let it sit at room temperature for 2-3 days to allow the essential oil to separate and mature.

remedies.remedy_desc: Valeriana officinalis essential oils contains valerenic acid, isovaleric acid, and valeranone as its main bioactive constituents. These compounds contribute to its sedative and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate hangover symptoms. Valerenic acid, in particular, is known for its ability to reduce stress and anxiety, while isovaleric acid has a calming effect on the nervous system. The anti-inflammatory properties of valerian root oil can also help reduce headaches and body aches commonly associated with hangovers. By reducing stress, calming the nervous system, and alleviating inflammation, Valeriana officinalis essential oils may offer relief from hangover symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Valeriana officinalis herbal essential oils for hangover
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Valeriana officinalis root, a carrier oil (like jojoba oil), and a glass jar.
Clean and dry the Valeriana officinalis root, then chop it into small pieces.
In a saucepan, combine 2 cups of water and the chopped root. Heat it on low for 2 hours, then let it cool.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into the glass jar. Discard the root. Add 1 tablespoon of carrier oil to the liquid.
Let the mixture sit for 2 days, then strain it again through cheesecloth into a new container. Use this liquid as your Valeriana officinalis essential oil.

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita essential oils contains compounds like menthone, menthol, and limonene. These active constituents have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which can help alleviate headaches and body aches often associated with hangovers. Menthol, in particular, is known for its ability to ease nausea and vomiting, making it a useful remedy for hangover symptoms. The oil's decongestant properties also help to clear congestion and sinus pressure, making it easier to breathe and feel more refreshed. By addressing these common hangover issues, Mentha x piperita essential oils can help alleviate hangover discomfort.
remedies.image_alt: Mentha x piperita herbal essential oils for hangover
remedy_procedure: Gather 2 cups of fresh Mentha x piperita leaves and 1 cup of water.
Chop the leaves finely and place them in a large bowl.
Pour the water over the leaves and let it sit for 2 hours to allow the mixture to infuse.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle. Discard the solids.
Add a tablespoon of carrier oil (like coconut or jojoba oil) to the liquid and shake the bottle well to mix.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis essential oils contains rosinic acid, bornyl acetate, and camphor as its main bioactive constituents. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate hangover symptoms. Rosmarinus officinalis essential oils also contains carnosic acid, a powerful antioxidant that can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body. The essential oil's properties can help relieve hangover symptoms such as headaches and nausea by reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation. Its expectorant properties can also help alleviate congestion and coughing associated with hangovers.
remedies.image_alt: Rosmarinus officinalis herbal essential oils for hangover
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Rosmarinus officinalis leaves and 1 cup of carrier oil (like coconut or olive oil)
Wash the leaves with cold water and pat them dry with a paper towel
Combine the leaves and carrier oil in a clean glass jar, making sure the leaves are completely covered
Steep the mixture in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day
Strain the oil through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle, discard the solids

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba essential oils contains bioactive constituents like flavonoids and terpenoids. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to reduce the severity of hangover symptoms. The flavonoids in Ginkgo biloba, particularly quercetin, help to protect the body from oxidative stress and inflammation caused by excessive alcohol consumption. Terpenoids, such as bilobalide, have a calming effect on the brain and nervous system, which can help to alleviate headaches and fatigue associated with hangovers. By reducing inflammation and oxidative stress, and promoting relaxation, Ginkgo biloba essential oils may help to alleviate hangover symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Ginkgo biloba herbal essential oils for hangover
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Ginkgo biloba leaves and 2 cups of carrier oil like coconut or olive oil.
Combine the Ginkgo biloba leaves and carrier oil in a clean glass jar. Stir well.
Steep the mixture in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar. Discard the solids.
Bottle the Ginkgo biloba essential oil and store it in a cool, dark place. Use 2-3 drops as needed to help alleviate hangover symptoms.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melissa officinalis essential oils contains boiactive constituents like linalool and linalyl acetate, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These properties help to soothe and calm the stomach, reducing nausea and vomiting associated with hangovers. Linalool in Melissa officinalis essential oils also has a sedative effect, which can help to reduce anxiety and promote relaxation. The antioxidant properties of Melissa officinalis essential oils can also help to protect the body from oxidative stress caused by alcohol consumption. By reducing inflammation, soothing the stomach, and promoting relaxation, Melissa officinalis essential oils can help to alleviate hangover symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Melissa officinalis herbal essential oils for hangover
remedy_procedure: Gather 2 cups of Melissa officinalis flowers and 1 cup of carrier oil like jojoba or sweet almond oil.
Combine the flowers and carrier oil in a clean glass jar. Let it sit for 2 weeks in a cool, dark place, shaking the jar every day.
After 2 weeks, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar. Discard the solids.
Transfer the liquid to a clean glass bottle and add 10 drops of vitamin E oil to preserve the oil. Close the bottle tightly.
Store the essential oil in a cool, dark place and use 5-7 drops in a diffuser or mix with a carrier oil for topical use to help alleviate hangover symptoms.

remedies.remedy_desc: Silybum marianum essential oils contains compounds like silymarin, silibinin, and silidianin. These bioactive constituents have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce the swelling and pain associated with hangovers. Silymarin also has antioxidant properties, which can neutralize free radicals that contribute to the oxidative stress that can occur after excessive alcohol consumption. Silibinin has been shown to have protective effects on the liver, which can help mitigate the damage caused by excessive drinking. By reducing inflammation, oxidative stress, and liver damage, Silybum marianum essential oils may provide relief from hangover symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Silybum marianum herbal essential oils for hangover
remedy_procedure: Gather 2 cups of Silybum marianum seeds and 1 cup of carrier oil (like jojoba or sweet almond oil).
Heat 1 cup of carrier oil in a saucepan over low heat (150-200°F).
Add 2 cups of Silybum marianum seeds to the carrier oil and stir well. Let it steep for 2-3 hours.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean container. Discard the solids.
Store the Silybum marianum essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid. Use 5-7 drops as needed to help alleviate hangover symptoms.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cymbopogon citratus essential oils contains a compound called limonene, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce the swelling and pain often associated with hangovers. It also contains beta-pinene, a compound that can help alleviate nausea and vomiting, common symptoms of a hangover. The essential oil's antimicrobial properties, thanks to its high content of citral, can help combat the dehydration often caused by excessive drinking. Additionally, the oil's ability to calm the nervous system, attributed to its linalool content, can help reduce the anxiety and stress that often accompany a hangover. By addressing these symptoms, Cymbopogon citratus essential oils can help alleviate the discomforts of a hangover.
remedies.image_alt: Cymbopogon citratus herbal essential oils for hangover
remedy_procedure: Gather materials: 1 cup of Cymbopogon citratus leaves, a clean glass jar, a coffee filter, a cheescloth, a bowl, and a carrier oil like coconut oil.
Wash the Cymbopogon citratus leaves in cold water and pat them dry with a paper towel.
Place the leaves in the glass jar and pour 1 cup of carrier oil over them. Cover the jar with a lid.
Let the mixture sit in a cool, dark place for 2 weeks, shaking the jar every day. Then, strain the mixture through a coffee filter into a bowl.
Filter the liquid again through a cheesecloth into another bowl, and discard the solids. Transfer the Cymbopogon citratus essential oil to a dark glass bottle and store it in a cool place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Thymus vulgaris essential oils contains thymol and carvacrol as its primary bioactive constituents. Thymol and carvacrol have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate hangover symptoms such as headaches and nausea. These compounds also possess antioxidant properties, helping to reduce oxidative stress caused by excessive alcohol consumption. Additionally, thymol has been shown to have analgesic properties, which can help alleviate hangover-related pain and discomfort. By reducing inflammation and alleviating pain, thymol and carvacrol in Thymus vulgaris essential oils may help alleviate some hangover symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Thymus vulgaris herbal essential oils for hangover
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Thymus vulgaris leaves and stems.
Use a clean glass container and add 1 cup of water. Add 1 tablespoon of glycerin.
Add the fresh Thymus vulgaris leaves and stems to the container. Make sure they are fully covered by the water.
Cover the container and let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2-3 days. This will allow the mixture to steep.
After the steeping process, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle. Discard the solids.

remedies.remedy_desc: Artemisia absinthium essential oils contains thujone, borneol, and camphor, which are key bioactive constituents that contribute to its potential hangover-relieving properties. Thujone, in particular, has anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects, which can help to alleviate headaches and stomach discomfort often associated with hangovers. Borneol has a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help to reduce nausea and dizziness. Camphor, on the other hand, has analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to ease muscle pain and cramping. By incorporating Artemisia absinthium essential oils into a recovery routine, individuals may experience relief from hangover symptoms due to its bioactive constituents and their corresponding properties.
remedies.image_alt: Artemisia absinthium herbal essential oils for hangover
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Artemisia absinthium leaves, 1 cup of water, a glass jar, a cheesecloth, and a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil.
Combine the fresh Artemisia absinthium leaves and water in the glass jar. Let it steep in a cool, dark place for 2-3 hours.
Strain the liquid through the cheesecloth into another container. Discard the solids.
Mix 1 tablespoon of the strained liquid with 1 cup of carrier oil in the glass jar. Stir well.
Let the mixture sit in a cool, dark place for 2-3 days. Shake the jar daily. After 2-3 days, filter the oil through a cheesecloth and store it in a dark glass bottle.

faq_prevent: Drinking herbal tea after a night out might help with hangover symptoms. Some herbal teas, like peppermint and ginger, have anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe a sore stomach and calm the mind. They may also help to rehydrate the body and alleviate headaches.
faq_safe: While herbal teas can help with hangover symptoms, consuming them daily for relief isn't recommended. Some herbs can interact with medications or worsen underlying health issues. For example, peppermint tea can relax muscles but also slow down digestion. Stomach problems or allergies might arise if you drink them regularly.
faq_timeline: Herbal teas can help alleviate hangover symptoms. Some people start feeling better within 30 minutes to an hour after drinking. The teas seem to work faster if consumed while still hungover. The effects might last a few hours, giving you some relief from headaches and nausea.
faq_time: Drinking herbal tea for a hangover can help ease symptoms.  It's best to have it in the morning when you wake up, as it can help replenish fluids and calm your stomach.  The caffeine in some teas can also help increase alertness and wakefulness.
faq_interaction: Some herbal teas might interact with prescription medications for hangover relief. For example, St. John's Wort can affect blood thinners, while ginseng can interact with blood pressure medications. It's best to talk to the pharmacist about any interactions before taking herbal teas with prescription medications.
faq_pregnant: It's generally best to avoid herbal teas for hangovers while pregnant, as some may contain caffeine or other substances that can harm the baby. Peppermint and ginger teas are often considered safe, but it's crucial to check the ingredients and ensure they're caffeine-free. Even then, moderation is key.
faq_breastfeeding: When breastfeeding, it's a good idea to be cautious with herbal teas, especially for hangover relief. Some herbs, like peppermint and ginger, are generally considered safe, but others, such as dandelion root and chamomile, might pass into breast milk. If you're unsure, choose teas that are known to be safe and in moderation.
title_2: 11 Herbal Remedies: Essential Oils for College Students with Hangover
